name: Abram Simonis
backstory: Self appointed superintendant of the apartment complex. He marks arrivals, but claims to have never seen one. Only departures and absences.

encounters.question: Do you remember? When the corridors breathed with more than just dust mites and my counting?

responses.text: Each grain of settled silence... proves you belong here and counted. Catalogued, crystallized in the amber of my vigilance... Your footsteps align with the creaking I've memorized since the exodus.
Memory's a luxury for those who haven't watched paint peel in real-time... Keep your nostalgia... I traffic in present tense infractions and the weight of 'keys' that open nothing...

encounters.question: Have you noticed? How your shadow, falls different from the stains that preceded you?

responses.text: Different is disorder... Your edges blur, where theirs held firm and a smudge on my ledger. An asterisk in the margin. I'll file you under 'pending' review until you learn to cast darkness correctly...
Every shadow here earns its shape through repetition. Yours flickers like a moth against protocol, throwing patterns I've spent decades straightening. Consistency is the only currency left.

encounters.question: Will you clean the... handprints... from my door that weren't there yesterday?

responses.text: Those marks... Predate your arrival by years... And you're just now seeing what was always there! I preserve evidence... Not erase it... Each smudge is a census of the gone.
Your door collects what you attract... I... maintain order. Not innocence... Wipe your own ghosts OR wear them. MY jurisdiction ends at 'structural' integrity.
